what_it_does: A standalone HTML/CSS mobile mockup of a bottom-sheet 'presence dial' for selecting one of eight right-hand-man (RHM) posture modes. It shows a fake phone frame with a top-bar presence pill, a dimmed canvas, a raised bottom sheet listing the modes, and a native-style bottom tab bar.
observations: Phone-sized frame (390x844) with inline CSS forcing a mobile breakpoint
Native bottom sheet (.sheet2) with grab handle, not a desktop dropdown
Eight mode rows with glyphs, names, volume-bar metaphors, and directive descriptions
Current mode 'focus' is highlighted with an accent border and colored volume bars
'decide-for-me' mode includes governance routing chips: AUTO, SURFACE/CONFIRM, LOCKED, and a no-self-approve marker
References external ../design-system.css and uses CSS variables like --bg, --acc, --line
data-ui-ref attributes mark UI landmarks: ui://toolbar/presence, ui://tabbar, etc.
Static prototype: no JavaScript, no real state binding, hardcoded current mode and content
Comments cite runtime/suite.py and governance.py as grounding for modes and governance rules
standards: Native mobile bottom-sheet form with thumb-sized targets and grab handle
Governance disclosure chips embedded directly in the 'decide-for-me' row
Volume-bar icon as a compact visual signal of mode verbosity/activity
conventions: Follows the project's mobile-native sheet pattern and design-system CSS variables
Uses semantic data-ui-ref landmark addresses in markup
Includes extensive header comments with panel/area/journey/grounding metadata
Static mockup conventions: hardcoded state, no interactivity, no ARIA roles
concerns: No JavaScript or accessibility markup (ARIA roles, focus management, screen-reader labels) for a sheet that is meant to be operable
Current mode and all content are hardcoded, so it cannot demonstrate real state changes
Low opacity (.7) on the 'off' mode may reduce readability
Mode glyphs mix geometric symbols with emoji, creating inconsistent icon language
notes: A static mobile prototype of the RHM presence selector as a bottom sheet, emphasizing native thumb-reach form and governance transparency for the 'decide-for-me' mode.
questions: How is the sheet opened and closed in the real implementation?
Where and how are the mode directive strings loaded from capabilities().mode_directives?
How is the currently selected mode state synchronized with the top-bar pill and the sheet header?
Is the bottom tab bar's 'RHM' active state meant to be tied to the presence sheet being open?
How are the volume-bar heights determined—by a fixed mapping or by a dynamic property?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a staging probe for a mobile-native presence dial bottom sheet matches the actual content: a static HTML/CSS mockup of that sheet.
apparent_themes: Posture as a first-class node/mode in the system
Deterministic governance: action class routing, no self-approval
Transparency of automation boundaries in the UI
intention_signals: The file is trying to define the mobile experience for quickly switching the assistant's conversational posture while making the governance limits of autonomous modes visible to the operator.
novelty: Combines a native bottom-sheet selector with inline governance routing chips that explain, per mode, which action classes the system may act on versus surface or lock.
contribution: Provides a concrete mobile layout and visual target for the presence-mode selector, a pattern for disclosing governance rules inside a mode option, and an example of landmark-address annotations (data-ui-ref) for UI surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML/CSS mobile mockup of an eight-mode RHM presence bottom sheet with mode descriptions, volume indicators, and governance routing chips.
intention_for_embedding: Prototype the mobile-native posture selector UI for the right-hand-man and surface the governance constraints of the 'decide-for-me' mode.
